cases.title: Client calls a service RPC and receives a successful typed response

given: a live Trellis runtime
the rpc fixture contracts are approved
when: the client calls Entity.Get through the generated RPC surface
then: the service receives the typed input
the client receives { id, found: true }

cases.title: Client receives a declared service RPC error through the result surface

cases.title: Annotated invalid RPC input returns SchemaValidationError without dispatching the handler

the RPC input schema contains annotated validation metadata for every failing constraint

the service handler is not called

cases.title: Mixed annotated and unannotated invalid RPC input returns ValidationError without dispatching the handler
cases.description: A client sends invalid input with both annotated and unannotated validation failures, receives ValidationError over the live RPC wire, and the service handler is not called.

the RPC input schema has one annotated failing constraint and one unannotated failing constraint
when: the client calls Validation.Mixed with invalid input
then: the client receives ValidationError over the RPC wire
the client does not receive SchemaValidationError
the service handler is not called

cases.title: RPC auth validation retries when the first live session lookup is transiently missing
cases.description: A live client RPC reaches a service while the client session row is transiently absent from the control-plane database; the first Auth.Requests.Validate returns session_not_found, the retry succeeds after the row is restored, and the service handler runs once.

a connected client and service

the saved session row is restored after the first Auth.Requests.Validate attempt is observed
then: the RPC succeeds after retry
the service handler runs once

then: Auth.Sessions.List still shows the same app session key
the app session createdAt is preserved
the app session reflects the updated contract authority
stale runtime connection presence from the old authority is removed

cases.title: Local login replaces a session when the authenticated identity changes
cases.description: An app client authenticates with local login, then the same session key completes a later local-login bind as a different local identity. Auth replaces the session principal and removes stale runtime access from the previous identity.

given: a registered service handles an RPC required by the app client contract
the app client has an authenticated approved session for one local identity
a second active local admin identity exists
when: the same app session key completes local login and approval as the second identity
then: Auth.Sessions.List shows the app session principal has changed
Auth.Sessions.Me on the replacement connection reports the second identity
the previous live connection is denied after replacement

cases.title: Auth.Sessions.Logout deletes the caller session and connection presence
cases.description: An authenticated app/client session calls Auth.Sessions.Logout with empty input through the generated RPC surface; the session row and matching runtime connection presence disappear, and the old session can no longer call Auth.Sessions.Me.

given: a registered service handles an RPC required by the app client contract
the app client has an authenticated approved session
Auth.Connections.List shows runtime connection presence for the app session
when: the app client calls Auth.Sessions.Logout with empty input
then: Auth.Sessions.List no longer shows the logged-out app session
Auth.Connections.List no longer shows runtime presence for the logged-out session
the existing app session is denied for subsequent Auth.Sessions.Me calls

cases.title: Auth.Sessions.Logout removes connection presence when kick fails
cases.description: An authenticated app/client session calls Auth.Sessions.Logout while the live kick path rejects; logout still succeeds and durable session and connection presence are removed.

given: the app client has an authenticated approved session
Auth.Connections.List shows runtime connection presence for the app session
the Trellis test hook makes the logout kick reject once
when: the app client calls Auth.Sessions.Logout with empty input
then: Auth.Sessions.Logout succeeds
Auth.Sessions.List no longer shows the logged-out app session
Auth.Connections.List no longer shows runtime presence for the logged-out session

cases.title: Auth.Sessions.Me reports the app user envelope
cases.description: An authenticated app/client session calls Auth.Sessions.Me through the generated RPC surface and receives app participant metadata with a live user envelope and null device/service envelopes.

given: the app client has an authenticated approved session
when: the app client calls Auth.Sessions.Me
then: the response participantKind is app
the response includes an active user envelope with current capabilities
the response device and service envelopes are null

cases.title: Auth list RPCs report participant metadata
cases.description: An admin app lists live app, agent, activated device, and service sessions and app/agent runtime connections through generated Auth.Sessions.List and Auth.Connections.List, proving participantKind and explicit principal metadata are present on list rows.

given: a registered service is connected
the app client and agent have authenticated approved sessions
a known device is provisioned and activated
when: the admin calls Auth.Sessions.List and Auth.Connections.List
then: Auth.Sessions.List includes app user participant metadata
Auth.Sessions.List includes agent user participant metadata
Auth.Sessions.List includes activated device participant metadata
Auth.Sessions.List includes service participant metadata
Auth.Connections.List includes app connection participant metadata
Auth.Connections.List includes agent connection participant metadata

cases.title: Auth.Connections.List skips malformed connection presence
cases.description: A malformed raw auth connection-presence entry is seeded beside a valid live app connection; Auth.Connections.List skips the malformed entry and still returns the valid connection.

given: the app client has an authenticated approved session
Auth.Connections.List shows one valid runtime connection for the app session
a test helper seeds one malformed raw connection presence entry for the same session
when: the admin calls Auth.Connections.List for the session
then: the malformed connection presence entry is skipped
the valid connection entry is still returned

cases.title: Auth.Sessions.Me reports service envelope and current user state
cases.description: An authenticated app session observes current SQL-backed user active/capability state through Auth.Sessions.Me, and a connected service observes the service envelope from durable service instance state even when the stored service session deployment is stale.

given: the app client has an authenticated approved session
a registered service is connected
when: the durable user projection is updated
the service session deployment is made stale while the durable service instance remains current
the app and service call Auth.Sessions.Me
then: Auth.Sessions.Me reports current user active and resolved capability state
Auth.Sessions.Me reports participantKind service with an active service envelope
the service envelope capabilities come from the current durable service instance

cases.title: Auth.Sessions.Me reports the activated device envelope
cases.description: An activated device session calls Auth.Sessions.Me and receives device participant metadata with the activated device envelope and activating user context.

given: a known device is provisioned and activated
when: the activated device calls Auth.Sessions.Me
then: the response participantKind is device
the response includes the activated device deployment and runtime public key
the response includes activating user context

cases.title: Auth.Sessions.Me rejects stale user session state
cases.description: An authenticated app session is made stale by deleting the durable session row and then the durable user projection; Auth.Sessions.Me rejects both stale user principal states.

given: the app client has an authenticated approved session
when: the session row is deleted
the user projection row is deleted
then: Auth.Sessions.Me rejects the deleted session
Auth.Sessions.Me rejects the session whose durable user projection is missing

cases.title: Auth.Sessions.Me rejects stale device session state
cases.description: An activated device session is made stale by deleting the durable session row, changing the device instance deployment, and changing the activation identity key; Auth.Sessions.Me rejects each stale device principal state.

when: the device session row is deleted
the device instance deployment no longer matches the activation
the activation identity key no longer matches the session

Auth.Sessions.Me rejects the stale activation deployment
Auth.Sessions.Me rejects the stale activation identity key

cases.title: Auth.Requests.Validate enforces live request proof validation
cases.description: A live service calls Auth.Requests.Validate for a live app session and service session, proving malformed payload hashes, stale proof timestamps, replayed request ids, missing-session replay behavior, delegated publish-subject checks, and current service-instance capabilities through the control-plane validator.

given: a registered service can call Auth.Requests.Validate
an app client has an authenticated approved session for one service RPC subject
when: the service validates signed app requests with valid, malformed, stale, replayed, denied-subject, and missing-session inputs
the service validates its own service session after current service-instance capabilities change
then: malformed payload hashes return invalid_signature
stale proofs return iat_out_of_range
replayed request ids return invalid_signature
missing sessions return session_not_found without consuming replay state
delegated publish subjects gate app validation
current service-instance capabilities gate service validation

cases.title: Revoked app session cannot continue or reconnect
cases.description: An authenticated app/client session can call Auth.Sessions.Me or a service RPC, an admin revokes the session through the public Auth sessions admin surface, subsequent calls or reconnect attempts are denied, and public Auth session state reflects the revocation.

the app client has an authenticated approved session

when: the admin revokes the app session through Auth.Sessions.Revoke
then: Auth.Sessions.List no longer shows the revoked app session
the existing app session is denied for subsequent Auth.Sessions.Me or service RPC calls
the app client cannot reconnect with the revoked session without completing a new auth flow

both sessions share one approved durable identity grant

then: the durable identity grant is deleted
Auth.Sessions.List no longer shows either sibling app session
Auth.Connections.List no longer shows runtime presence for either sibling app session
both existing app sessions are denied for subsequent Auth.Sessions.Me calls

cases.title: Auth.Sessions.Revoke cascades agent grant revocation

both sessions share one approved durable identity grant

when: the admin revokes one agent session through Auth.Sessions.Revoke
then: the durable identity grant is deleted
Auth.Sessions.List no longer shows either sibling agent session

both existing agent sessions are denied for subsequent Auth.Sessions.Me calls

cases.title: Auth.Sessions.Revoke revokes device and service runtime access
cases.description: Revoking a live device session through Auth.Sessions.Revoke revokes the durable device activation and blocks device reconnect. Revoking a live service session through Auth.Sessions.Revoke disables the durable service instance and blocks service reconnect.

a provisioned service instance is connected with a live service session

the admin revokes the service session through Auth.Sessions.Revoke

the device cannot reconnect with the same root secret
the durable service instance is listed as disabled
the old service session is removed
the service cannot reconnect with the same instance key

cases.description: A service handler uses createSqlOutbox in an RPC handler, enqueues an event inside the transaction, and the event is published to NATS after the SQL transaction commits. The event is observable through a live event capture.

the Document.Audited event derived by the listener is captured

the SQL outbox row has kind 'event.publish'

a pending legacy outbox row stores its event name in the event column

the service enqueues through service.createSqlOutbox
then: the legacy row is preserved with kind 'event.publish'
the legacy event value is available as name
the 0.11 SQL outbox insert succeeds

typescript.testName: control-plane.catalog-dependency-issue-resolved-by-provider resolves an app dependency issue when a provider contract appears

cases.title: Catalog force replace resolves a catalog issue
cases.description: Invokes the catalog issue resolver against incompatible active offers and verifies force-replace makes the replacement digest active.

given: The catalog contains incompatible active offers and reports an issue.
when: An admin resolves the issue with force-replace.
then: The current effective offers are withdrawn, the replacement digest becomes active, and the catalog issue clears.

typescript.testName: control-plane.catalog-force-replace-resolves-catalog-issue resolves incompatible active catalog issue with force-replace

cases.title: Catalog Contract.Get and Surface.Status report provider runtime state
cases.description: Uses generated Core Contract.Get, Core Surface.Status, and Auth admin RPCs against live services to verify catalog manifest projection, edge statuses, validation failures, no-live filtering, same-contract old-digest filtering, unrelated-live-service filtering, and provider runtime states.

participants.name: trellis control plane
participants.contract: Trellis Core Surface.Status and Auth admin RPC

participants.name: authorized client app
participants.contract: case-scoped app contract with Core Surface.Status and provider RPC authority

participants.name: observer client app
participants.contract: case-scoped app contract with Core Surface.Status but no provider RPC authority

given: A documented provider service contract shape is approved before the provider is active.
when: An authorized client calls generated Trellis.Contract.Get for the provider digest.
An authorized client calls generated Trellis.Surface.Status for unknown and invalid surface requests.
An authorized client calls generated Trellis.Surface.Status before and after the provider connects.
An observer lacking provider RPC authority calls generated Trellis.Surface.Status.
The provider live connection is kicked through generated Auth connection RPCs.
An older same-contract provider digest without the requested surface connects while the provider remains no-live.
An unrelated service for another contract connects while the provider remains no-live.
The provider service instance is disabled through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: Contract.Get preserves canonical exports and contract, RPC, operation, and event docs.
Surface.Status reports unknown contract and unknown surface responses and rejects invalid actions.
Surface.Status reports the public pre-provider state.
Surface.Status reports the provider as available and live after connection.
Surface.Status reports valid event publish and feed subscribe requests as available when the provider is live.
Surface.Status reports unauthorized with the provider capability for the observer.
Surface.Status reports the provider as available without a live implementer after the provider connection is kicked.
Surface.Status keeps reporting the provider as no-live when an older same-contract digest without the requested surface is live.
Surface.Status keeps reporting the provider as no-live when an unrelated service is live.
Surface.Status reports the disabled provider runtime state after instance disable.

typescript.testName: control-plane.catalog-surface-status-reports-provider-runtime reports provider runtime through Surface.Status

cases.title: Jobs admin lists and cancels a service-local job
cases.description: Starts a service-local job and uses generated Jobs admin RPCs to observe and cancel it through the control plane.

given: A service-local long-running job is active.
when: An admin lists, gets, and cancels the job through generated Jobs RPCs.
then: The job is observable through Jobs admin and reaches the cancelled state.

typescript.testName: control-plane.jobs-admin-lists-and-cancels-job observes and cancels a service-local job

cases.title: Auth admin rolls back service deployment fault edges
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs and one-shot trellis-test hooks to verify service deployment create-authority rollback and cascade removal rollback with a provisioned service instance, then retries successfully.

given: A live Trellis control plane has create-authority and cascade-delete fail-once hooks enabled for service deployments.
when: The admin creates a service deployment, provisions a service instance, and removes the deployment with cascade through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: The create-authority fault leaves no deployment, the cascade-delete fault restores a deleted service instance while the deployment remains available, and the retry removes both records.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-service-deployment-rollback-fault rolls back service admin faults and retries successfully

cases.title: Auth admin rolls back device deployment fault edges
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs and one-shot trellis-test hooks to verify device deployment create-authority rollback and cascade removal rollback with a provisioned device, then retries successfully.

given: A live Trellis control plane has create-authority and cascade-delete fail-once hooks enabled for device deployments.
when: The admin creates a device deployment, provisions a device, and removes the deployment with cascade through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: The create-authority fault leaves no deployment, the cascade-delete fault restores a deleted device while the deployment remains available, and the retry removes both records.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-device-deployment-rollback-fault rolls back device admin faults and retries successfully

cases.title: Auth admin validates service deployment disable before persisting or kicking
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs, a live ping service/client, and a one-shot trellis-test validation hook to verify a failed service deployment disable validation leaves the deployment enabled and the connected service callable.

given: A live Trellis control plane has an enabled service deployment with a connected service and ping client.
The service deployment validateActiveCatalog fail-once hook is enabled.
when: The admin disables the service deployment through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: Disable returns the injected validation error.
Auth.Deployments.List still shows the deployment enabled.
The already connected client can still call the already connected service.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-service-deployment-validate-before-persist-kick validates before persisting disabled state or kicking service

cases.title: Auth admin rolls back service deployment disable refresh failure
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs and a one-shot trellis-test refresh hook to verify failed service deployment disable leaves the public deployment enabled.

given: A live Trellis control plane has the service deployment refresh fail-once hook enabled.
when: The admin creates a service deployment and disables it through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: Disable returns an error and Auth.Deployments.List still shows the deployment enabled.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-service-deployment-disable-refresh-rollback rolls back failed service deployment disable refresh

cases.title: Auth admin rolls back service deployment enable refresh failure
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs and a one-shot trellis-test refresh hook to verify failed service deployment enable leaves the public deployment disabled.

given: A live Trellis control plane has a disabled service deployment and the service deployment refresh fail-once hook enabled.
when: The admin enables the service deployment through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: Enable returns an error and Auth.Deployments.List still shows the deployment disabled.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-service-deployment-enable-refresh-rollback rolls back failed service deployment enable refresh

cases.title: Auth admin rolls back service instance disable refresh failure
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs and a one-shot trellis-test refresh hook to verify failed service instance disable leaves the instance enabled.

given: A live Trellis control plane has a provisioned service instance and the service instance refresh fail-once hook enabled.
when: The admin disables the service instance through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: Disable returns an error and Auth.ServiceInstances.List still shows the instance enabled.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-service-instance-disable-refresh-rollback rolls back failed service instance disable refresh

cases.title: Auth admin rolls back service instance enable refresh failure
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs and a one-shot trellis-test refresh hook to verify failed service instance enable leaves the instance disabled.

given: A live Trellis control plane has a disabled service instance and the service instance refresh fail-once hook enabled.
when: The admin enables the service instance through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: Enable returns an error and Auth.ServiceInstances.List still shows the instance disabled.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-service-instance-enable-refresh-rollback rolls back failed service instance enable refresh

cases.title: Auth admin rolls back service instance remove refresh failure
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs and a one-shot trellis-test refresh hook to verify failed service instance remove leaves the instance present.

given: A live Trellis control plane has a provisioned service instance and the service instance refresh fail-once hook enabled.
when: The admin removes the service instance through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: Remove returns an error and Auth.ServiceInstances.List still shows the instance present.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-service-instance-remove-refresh-rollback rolls back failed service instance remove refresh

cases.title: Auth admin rolls back device deployment disable refresh failure
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs and a one-shot trellis-test refresh hook to verify failed device deployment disable leaves the public deployment enabled.

given: A live Trellis control plane has the device deployment refresh fail-once hook enabled.
when: The admin creates a device deployment and disables it through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: Disable returns an error and Auth.Deployments.List still shows the deployment enabled.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-device-deployment-disable-refresh-rollback rolls back failed device deployment disable refresh

cases.title: Auth admin rolls back device deployment enable refresh failure
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs and a one-shot trellis-test refresh hook to verify failed device deployment enable leaves the public deployment disabled.

given: A live Trellis control plane has a disabled device deployment and the device deployment refresh fail-once hook enabled.
when: The admin enables the device deployment through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: Enable returns an error and Auth.Deployments.List still shows the deployment disabled.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-device-deployment-enable-refresh-rollback rolls back failed device deployment enable refresh

cases.title: Auth admin rolls back device instance disable refresh failure
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs and a one-shot trellis-test refresh hook to verify failed device instance disable leaves the device registered.

given: A live Trellis control plane has a provisioned device and the device instance refresh fail-once hook enabled.
when: The admin disables the device through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: Disable returns an error and Auth.Devices.List still shows the device registered.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-device-instance-disable-refresh-rollback rolls back failed device instance disable refresh

cases.title: Auth admin rolls back device instance enable refresh failure
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs and a one-shot trellis-test refresh hook to verify failed device instance enable leaves the device disabled.

given: A live Trellis control plane has a disabled device and the device instance refresh fail-once hook enabled.
when: The admin enables the device through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: Enable returns an error and Auth.Devices.List still shows the device disabled.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-device-instance-enable-refresh-rollback rolls back failed device instance enable refresh

cases.title: Auth admin rolls back device instance remove refresh failure
cases.description: Uses generated Auth admin RPCs and a one-shot trellis-test refresh hook to verify failed device instance remove leaves the device present.

given: A live Trellis control plane has a provisioned device and the device instance refresh fail-once hook enabled.
when: The admin removes the device through generated Auth admin RPCs.
then: Remove returns an error and Auth.Devices.List still shows the device present.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-device-instance-remove-refresh-rollback rolls back failed device instance remove refresh

cases.title: Auth admin manages service deployments through generated RPCs
cases.description: Uses generated Auth.Deployments admin RPCs to create, list, disable, enable, remove, and reject a second remove for a service deployment through the live control plane.

given: A live Trellis control plane has an authenticated local admin client.
when: The admin creates, lists, disables, enables, and removes a service deployment through generated Auth.Deployments RPCs.
then: The deployment lifecycle state is visible through list responses, removal succeeds once, and removing the missing deployment returns a modeled failure.

typescript.testName: control-plane.admin-service-deployment-lifecycle manages service deployments through generated Auth RPCs

cases.title: Durable listen without a declared group returns an error
cases.description: Connects a service that subscribes to a dependency event without declaring a durable group and verifies durable listen is rejected.

given: A consumer contract uses a source event but declares no durable consumer group.
when: The service attempts a durable listen for that dependency event.
then: The listen call returns a modeled error.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.durable-listen-without-declared-group-returns-err

cases.title: Ambiguous durable group requires opts.group
cases.description: Declares two durable groups for one event, verifies an unspecified group is rejected, and verifies the selected group works.

given: A dependency event has multiple declared durable consumer groups.
when: The service listens without opts.group and then with an explicit group.
then: The ambiguous listen is rejected and the explicit group listen succeeds.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.ambiguous-group-without-opts-group-returns-err-and-specifying-group-works

cases.title: Caller-provided durable name returns an error
cases.description: Verifies durable listener callers cannot override Trellis-provisioned durable consumer names.

given: Trellis provisions a durable consumer for the declared group.
when: The service attempts to pass its own durable name.
then: The listen call returns a modeled error.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.caller-provided-durable-name-returns-err

cases.title: Bound dependency consumer uses only the Trellis-provisioned consumer
cases.description: Verifies a dependency durable listener attaches to the Trellis-provisioned JetStream consumer without creating extra consumers.

given: A dependency durable consumer binding has been provisioned by Trellis.
when: The service listens and a publisher emits a source event.
then: The event is delivered through the provisioned consumer and no extra JetStream consumer is created.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.bound-dependency-consumer-uses-trellis-provisioned-consumer-only

cases.title: Ephemeral listener avoids durable metadata and JetStream consumer creation
cases.description: Listens ephemerally to a dependency event and verifies durable metadata and durable JetStream consumer creation are avoided.

given: A service can subscribe to a dependency event without selecting a durable group.
when: The service listens ephemerally and a publisher emits an event.
then: The event is delivered without durable metadata or an extra JetStream consumer.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.ephemeral-listener-avoids-durable-metadata-and-jetstream-consumer

cases.title: Duplicate handlers share a single group waiter
cases.description: Registers two handlers on the same durable group and verifies they share one active pull waiter while both receive the event.

given: A service registers duplicate handlers for the same dependency durable group.
when: A publisher emits a source event.
then: Both handlers observe the event while JetStream shows one active group waiter.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.duplicate-handlers-share-single-group-waiter

cases.title: Self-owned durable consumer receives a self-published event
cases.description: Publishes a service-owned event and verifies the same service receives it through its self-owned durable consumer.

given: A service declares a durable consumer for its own event.
when: The service publishes that self-owned event.
then: The service receives the event through the self-owned durable consumer.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.self-owned-durable-consumer-receives-self-published-event

cases.title: Grouped consumer waits for all dependency handlers before consuming a queued event
cases.description: Queues dependency events and verifies grouped delivery waits until all declared handlers for the group are ready.

given: A dependency durable group covers multiple event handlers and an event is queued.
when: The service registers the full handler set for the group.
then: Queued delivery begins only after all group handlers are ready.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.grouped-consumer-waits-for-all-handlers-before-consuming-queued-event

cases.title: Self-owned grouped consumer waits for all handlers before consuming a queued event
cases.description: Queues self-owned events and verifies grouped delivery waits until all self-event handlers for the durable group are ready.

given: A self-owned durable group covers multiple event handlers and an event is queued.
when: The service registers the full self-event handler set for the group.
then: Queued delivery begins only after all self-owned group handlers are ready.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.self-owned-grouped-consumer-waits-for-all-handlers-before-consuming-queued-event

cases.title: Abort and re-register restarts durable delivery
cases.description: Aborts a durable listener, waits for delivery to stop, re-registers the handler, and verifies queued delivery restarts.

given: A durable listener has been aborted after initial registration.
when: A queued event exists and the listener is re-registered.
then: Durable delivery resumes and the queued event is observed.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.abort-re-register-restarts-delivery

cases.title: Stop teardown stops durable delivery
cases.description: Stops a service with an active durable listener and verifies delivery does not continue after teardown.

given: A durable dependency listener is active.
when: The service stops and another event is published.
then: The stopped service does not receive additional durable deliveries.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.stop-teardown-stops-durable-delivery

cases.title: Transient missing consumer retries after reconcile
cases.description: Deletes a Trellis-provisioned JetStream consumer, verifies transient listen failure, reconciles, and verifies delivery recovers.

given: A Trellis-provisioned dependency durable consumer is transiently missing.
when: Trellis reconciles the consumer and the service listens again.
then: The durable listener recovers and receives the next event.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.transient-missing-consumer-retries-after-reconcile

cases.title: Readiness lost does not NAK an already delivered group message
cases.description: Forces readiness loss after a grouped durable message is delivered and verifies the delivered message is not negatively acknowledged.

given: A grouped durable message has already been delivered to a handler.
when: Group readiness is lost before the handler completes.
then: The delivered message is not NAKed solely because readiness changed.

typescript.testName: event-consumers.readiness-lost-does-not-nak-delivered-group-message

cases.title: Prepared publish preserves custom headers and annotates handler error
cases.description: Publishes a prepared event with custom headers and verifies handler error annotation preserves the prepared header metadata.

given: A service handler throws while processing a prepared event with custom headers.
when: The client publishes the prepared event.
then: The handler error annotation includes the prepared event header metadata.

typescript.testName: prepared-events.prepared-publish-preserves-custom-headers-and-annotates-handler-error publishes prepared headers into handler error annotation
